segments.text: ओम्नमो भगवते वासुदेवाया ददर्शातत्राभिजतं धराधारं फ्रोनियमानाविनम अग्रतम्ष्त्रया

Translation and Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shilaprabhu Paat
He saw there the all-powerful personality of Godhead
In a previous chapter we have discussed the incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead as Varaha, the boar.
While Varaha, with his tusks, engaged in uplifting the submerged earth from the depths of the water, this great demon, Hiranyaksha, met him and challenged him, calling him a beast.
Demons cannot understand the incarnations of the Lord. They think that his incarnations of fish or boar or tortoise are big beasts only.
They misunderstand the body of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, even in its human form,
and they direct their attention to the body of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
In the Chetanaya Sampradaya, there is sometimes a demoniac misconception that the descent of Nityananda's Prabhu.
Nityananda Prabhu's body is spiritual, but demoniac persons consider the body of the Supreme Personality to be material, just like ours.
Persons who have no intelligence deride the transcendental form of the Lord as material.
In this verse, Hiranyaksha saw that all-powerful Personality of Godhead, in his boar incarnation, bearing the earth upward on the ends of his tusks.
So we know, when the Lord descends in this world, he has two major functions.
